brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +35.4% since Feb 27 — Brent eases to ~$96 (Apr 10) as ceasefire holds on Iran front but Israel continues 50+ strikes in Lebanon — ADNOC CEO: 'Strait of Hormuz is not open' — Vance departs for Islamabad
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 8 close: $94.75 (-14% ceasefire crash). Apr 9 close: ~$97.50 (rebound). Apr 10 intraday: ~$95.92-96.30 — easing as ceasefire on Iran front holds ~72 hours but Hormuz remains effectively closed. ADNOC CEO Sultan Al Jaber (Apr 9-10): 'The Strait of Hormuz is not open. Access is being restricted, conditioned and controlled.' Only ~15 ships transited in first 48 hours vs ~110/day pre-war. 325 loaded tankers stranded (Lloyd's List). Iran planning tolls exceeding $1M/ship. Brent-WTI spread narrowing. Dated Brent hit all-time $144.42/bbl on Apr 7. VP Vance departed for Islamabad Apr 10 for highest-level US-Iran engagement since 1979. March CPI released this morning — energy-driven inflation expected. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / ADNOC / Lloyd's List / EIA.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +47.7% since Feb 27 — WTI at ~$98 (Apr 10) — ceasefire holding on Iran front ~72 hrs but Hormuz still closed — 325 loaded tankers stranded — only 15 ships through in 48 hrs
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 8 close: $94.41 (-16%, biggest drop since April 2020). Apr 9 close: ~$97.50 (rebound). Apr 10 intraday: ~$97.87-98.55. WTI touched $100 intraday Apr 9 before settling. $12-18 'Hormuz risk premium' persists as Iran maintains effective blockade. ADNOC CEO Al Jaber: 'The Strait of Hormuz is not open.' Defense Secretary Hegseth contradicted, claiming 'the strait is open.' Iran demanding military approval for each vessel and planning tolls exceeding $1M/ship. Iran released map indicating possible mining with new designated shipping lanes. US Navy decommissioned last 4 Avenger-class minesweepers from Bahrain Jan 2026. Gas $4.166/gal nationally (AAA Apr 9). Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / ADNOC / WSJ.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.186 / +39.8% since Feb 27 — $4.166 nationally (Apr 9 AAA) — crossed $4 on Apr 2 — California $5.89 — EIA forecasts peak ~$4.30 in April — surged 40% in 5 weeks
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 9: $4.166/gal nationally (AAA). Gas crossed $4/gallon on April 2, first time since August 2022. Surged from $2.98 to $4.16+ in just five weeks — a $1.18 increase (~40%). EIA forecasts retail gasoline peaking near $4.30/gal monthly average in April 2026. California leads at $5.89/gal. Diesel at ~$5.40-5.68/gal nationally. AEI estimates indirect cost to American consumers: gasoline/diesel spikes cost $12.1 billion ($92/household), jet fuel increases added $2.2 billion, farmers paid $131 million extra in fertilizer. Gasoline lags crude oil by 1-2 weeks — Apr 8 crude crash may not fully reach pumps given Apr 9-10 rebound. Source: AAA / EIA / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -54 pts / -0.8% from pre-war — S&P 6,824.66 (Apr 9 close, +0.62%) — VIX ~19.4-21.0 — March CPI released today — Vance en route to Islamabad
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 9 close: 6,824.66 (+0.62%) — partially recovering from Apr 8's ceasefire rally high of 6,782.81 (+2.51%). Dow ~47,910. Nasdaq ~22,635. VIX: ~19.40-21.04, down from 35+ pre-ceasefire. Gold: $4,743-4,782/oz (-0.9%). 10Y Treasury: 4.287-4.293%. March CPI released Apr 10 8:30 AM ET — Feb reading showed 2.4% YoY, March expected higher on energy-driven inflation. LMT ~$627.70 (+26% YTD, all-time high $676.70 Mar 2). RTX $203.19 (+10.79% YTD). XOM +28% YTD. CVX +24% YTD, market cap ~$396.8B. DAL +3.75% on ceasefire day. UAL ~$97.53 (+9.5% on ceasefire day, -13.4% YTD). JP Morgan Apr 9: 'ceasefire looks fleeting but direction positive' — estimates 4 months for full oil market recovery. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / JP Morgan.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$800M/day — CEASEFIRE ~72 HOURS — full force posture maintained — Bush CSG arriving theater — Vance en route to Islamabad — no official ceasefire burn rate released
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 42: ~$800M/day — ceasefire reduces active combat costs but CENTCOM maintains full force posture. No official 'ceasefire burn rate' released. Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes estimates $2B/day when including all operational costs, calling it 'the tip of the iceberg.' CSIS: ~$30B total to date. AEI: $22.3-31B through five weeks. ~50,000+ US service members participated in Operation Epic Fury. Bush CSG (CVN-77) arriving theater Apr 10-13 — third carrier committed. Total battle force: 291 ships (233 USS, 58 USNS), 108 deployed. Pentagon initially requested $200B supplemental; now expected to ask $80-100B (WaPo Apr 7). 60-day War Powers deadline: April 29. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / WaPo / USNI Fleet Tracker.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (42 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$49.3B through Day 42 — CSIS: ~$30B — AEI: $22.3-31B — Harvard: $2B/day, $1 trillion long-term — WarCosts.org: $35B ($236/taxpayer) — Pentagon supplemental shrunk from $200B to $80-100B
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12, ~$30B total to date. AEI: $22.3-31B for five weeks. Harvard Prof. Bilmes: $2B/day current rate, $1 trillion long-term — 'the tip of the iceberg.' WarCosts.org: $35B ($236/taxpayer). First 100 hours cost $3.7B (~$891M/day). Pentagon supplemental shrunk from initial $200B request to expected $80-100B (WaPo Apr 7). FY2027 budget includes 150% increase ($47B boost) in munitions procurement. Supplemental faces bipartisan opposition. Trump approval: CNN/SSRS 35% overall, 31% on economy (career low). Silver Bulletin net -16.3 (all-time 2nd-term low: -17.5). YouGov: 37% approve/56% disapprove (net -19). 60-day War Powers deadline: April 29. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / AEI / Harvard / WarCosts.org / WaPo / CNN / YouGov / Silver Bulletin.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE ~72 HRS — 35,500+ targets cumulative — CENTCOM: 13,000+ targets, 155+ warships sunk (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses destroyed — 50,000+ US service members participated — Iran air force: zero flights/day
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper Apr 9: in fewer than 40 days, 13,000+ strikes destroyed 'the military that Iran built for over 40 years' — including 155+ warships and submarines, the largest elimination of a navy since World War II. 80% of Iran's air defenses destroyed; 450 ballistic missile storage facilities; 800 drone storage facilities; 90% of weapons factories; every submarine sunk; 97% of naval mines eliminated. Iran air force at zero operational flights/day. ~50,000+ US service members participated in Operation Epic Fury. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,500+ through Day 40. Ceasefire holding on Iranian territory ~72 hours as of Apr 10 morning. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7. Israel continues operations in Lebanon: 50+ strikes in 24 hours on Apr 10 with 'marked reduction' in Beirut targeting due to US pressure. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / USNI.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 US KIA (CENTCOM confirmed) — 373+ WIA (330 returned to duty, 5 seriously wounded) — Intercept: Pentagon may be undercounting — named KIA: Sgt. Pennington, SFC Tietjens, Capt. Khork — ceasefire ~72 hrs
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. CENTCOM confirmed: 13 KIA, 373+ WIA. Of the wounded, 330 returned to duty and 5 remain seriously wounded. The Intercept reported the Pentagon may be undercounting — DCAS lists 303 wounded but excludes 200+ sailors treated after the USS Ford fire on March 12. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Named KIA include Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, and Capt. Cody Khork. 6 soldiers killed at Port Shuaiba Kuwait (Mar 1), 6 airmen in KC-135 crash over Iraq (Mar 12), 1 in Saudi Arabia. F-15E crew shot down Apr 3 fully rescued. A-10 pilot also recovered. Total aircraft losses: 7 airframes + 12+ MQ-9 Reaper drones. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~72 hours). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: CENTCOM: 373+ WIA — 330 returned to duty — 5 seriously wounded — Intercept: Pentagon undercounting (excludes 200+ Ford fire sailors) — most injuries TBI from blast overpressure — ceasefire ~72 hrs
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM confirmed: 373+ WIA. Of those, 330 returned to duty and 5 remain seriously wounded. The Intercept April 8 report: Pentagon may be undercounting — the DCAS lists 303 wounded but excludes 200+ sailors treated after the USS Ford fire on March 12. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Two-week ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~72 hours as of Apr 10 morning). No confirmed new WIA since ceasefire. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / The Intercept.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076 / HRANA Apr 7: 3,636 (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 714 unclassified) / Iran International: 4,700+ security forces / Hengaw: 5,300+ military — spread of 3,200+ deaths between sources — 15% under 18 — 3.2M displaced
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources. Iran Health Ministry: 2,076 killed, 26,500+ injured (conservative official count). HRANA (Apr 7): 3,636 deaths — 1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 714 unclassified. HRANA notes 15% of casualties were under 18. Iran International: 4,700+ security forces killed (Mar 31). Hengaw: 5,300+ military forces killed (Mar 18). Spread between Iran's official figures (2,076) and independent trackers (3,636-5,300+) is significant. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime after satellite imagery analysis; Lamerd sports hall strike (21 killed including 4 children); Karaj bridge attack (Apr 2, 8 civilians). Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. 307 health/medical facilities damaged (IRCS). 25 hospitals damaged, 9 out of service. 3.2M displaced; 100,000 left Tehran in first two days. UNHCR: $80M Flash Refugee Response Plan. 1.65M Afghan refugees inside Iran especially vulnerable; 35,000+ Afghans returned since war began. Ceasefire holding on Iranian territory ~72 hours. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / Hengaw / Iran International / WHO / OCHA / UNHCR / HRW.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,888+ killed, 6,092 injured, ~1M displaced — Israel conducts 50+ strikes in 24 hrs on Apr 10 (reduced Beirut targeting due to US pressure) — Ali Yusuf Harshi (Qassem's nephew) killed Apr 9 — UNICEF: 600 children killed or injured — 5 IDF divisions in southern Lebanon — all Litani bridges destroyed
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Total: 1,888+ killed, 6,092 injured, ~1 million displaced. UNICEF reports 600 children killed or injured. DEADLIEST DAY: April 8 'Operation Eternal Darkness' — 254 killed, 1,165+ wounded. On April 9, Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs and killed Ali Yusuf Harshi — nephew and personal secretary of Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem — in Tallet Khayat. By April 10, Israel had conducted 50+ additional strikes in 24 hours, though with a 'marked reduction' in Beirut targeting attributed to direct US pressure. Trump told NBC he asked Netanyahu to be 'a little more low-key' in Lebanon. Hezbollah resumed rocket fire at Kiryat Shmona, Metula, Misgav Am on Apr 9 after initially committing to ceasefire. IDF has 5 divisions operating in southern Lebanon and has destroyed all main bridges over the Litani River (Mar 12-Apr 8 per HRW). WHO warned Lebanese hospitals may exhaust vital medical supplies within days. WHO verified 92 attacks on health facilities in Lebanon. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / IDF / UNICEF / WHO / HRW / UN Human Rights.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: UNHCR: 7.5M+ — Iran 3.2M IDPs (100,000 left Tehran in first 2 days) — Lebanon 1M+ (700K in shelters) — UNHCR $80M Flash Response Plan covering 2.8M across 6 countries — 1.65M Afghan refugees in Iran especially vulnerable — 35,000+ Afghans returned
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR: up to 3.2 million people displaced internally in Iran. 100,000 left Tehran in the first two days. UNHCR launched $80 million Flash Refugee Response Plan covering 2.8 million people across six countries. Lebanon: ~1 million displaced. 1.65 million Afghan refugees inside Iran are especially vulnerable. 35,000+ Afghans returned to Afghanistan since war began. Ceasefire holding on Iran may slow displacement but Lebanon excluded from ceasefire per Netanyahu — continued Israeli strikes. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — Hormuz still effectively closed — only ~15 ships in 48 hrs vs 110/day pre-war — 600+ vessels stranded including 325 tankers (Lloyd's) — ~20,000 seafarers stuck — ADNOC CEO: 'not open'
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz: ADNOC CEO Sultan Al Jaber stated bluntly Apr 9-10: 'The Strait of Hormuz is not open. Access is being restricted, conditioned and controlled.' Only ~15 ships transited in first 48 hours of ceasefire vs pre-war ~110/day. 600+ vessels stranded inside Gulf including 325 tankers (Lloyd's List Intelligence) with ~20,000 seafarers. Defense Secretary Hegseth contradicted ADNOC, claiming 'the strait is open.' Iran demanding military approval for each vessel and planning tolls exceeding $1 million per ship. Iran released map indicating possible mining with designated shipping lanes. US Navy decommissioned last 4 Avenger-class minesweepers from Bahrain Jan 2026; Navy escorts 'aren't currently feasible' (WSJ). Source: ADNOC / Lloyd's List / IMO / IATA / WSJ / Bloomberg.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA Iran: 244+ (15% of casualties under 18) — UNICEF Lebanon: 600 children killed or injured — Minab school: ~175 killed incl. schoolchildren — Lamerd sports hall: 4 children — Karaj bridge: civilians celebrating Sizdah Be-dar — HRW: Minab = war crime
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran — HRANA notes 15% of casualties were under 18. UNICEF reports 600 children killed or injured in Lebanon (combined figure). Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime after satellite imagery analysis; Lamerd sports hall strike (21 killed including 4 children); Karaj bridge attack (Apr 2, 8 civilians celebrating Sizdah Be-dar). WHO verified 20+ attacks on healthcare in Iran (9 deaths). 763 schools damaged, 65+ destroyed. 30+ universities struck. Ceasefire holding on Iranian territory ~72 hours. Israel continues Lebanon operations — 50+ strikes Apr 10. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / WHO / HRW / Amnesty International.
